title: Using IF and Logical Functions
description: Use IF statements to automate decisions in spreadsheets, such as grading student scores or calculating employee bonuses.
objectives: Use nested IF for multiple conditions
Combine IF with AND and OR
Automate grade remarks and bonus decisions
content:
Automating Decisions with IF

In the workplace, you often need to make decisions based on numbers. Should a student pass or fail? Should an employee receive a bonus? Is a stock level too low? The IF function lets Excel make these decisions automatically based on the data you provide.

Basic IF Statement

The IF function has three parts: =IF(condition, value_if_true, value_if_false). For example: =IF(B2>=50, 'PASS', 'FAIL') checks if the score in B2 is 50 or above. If yes, it displays 'PASS'. If no, it displays 'FAIL'.

Nested IF for Multiple Conditions

What if you need more than two outcomes? Use nested IF. For example, to assign grades:

=IF(B2>=80, 'A', IF(B2>=70, 'B', IF(B2>=50, 'C', 'F')))

This checks: if 80 or above = A, else if 70 or above = B, else if 50 or above = C, else = F.

IF with AND and OR

=IF(AND(B2>=50, C2>=50), 'PASS', 'FAIL') means the student must pass BOTH subjects. =IF(OR(B2>=50, C2>=50), 'PASS', 'FAIL') means the student passes if they pass EITHER subject.

Student Grade Analysis Example

NameGenderCA1CA2CA3TotalAverageRemark
JonesF1226104816.0CAN DO BETTER
AndersonM3021308127.0GOOD WORK

Formulas:

  • Total: =SUM(C2:E2)
  • Average: =AVERAGE(C2:E2)
  • Remark: =IF(G2>26, 'GOOD WORK', 'CAN DO BETTER')

Workplace Scenario

You are the administrative assistant at a training college. At the end of each term, you must prepare a grade report for 50 students. Instead of manually deciding 'PASS' or 'FAIL' for each student, you write one IF formula. Excel automatically fills in the correct remark for every student. You also use COUNTIF to count how many students are male and how many are female, and how many passed overall. The principal receives a complete, accurate report in minutes instead of hours.

downloads.title: Grade Analysis Template
downloads.description: Excel template with IF remarks and SUM/AVERAGE

exercise: Create a spreadsheet with 10 students, columns: Name, Gender, CA1, CA2, CA3, CA4, CA5, Total, Average, Remark. Use SUM for Total, AVERAGE for Average, and IF for Remark (if Average > 26 then 'GOOD WORK', else 'CAN DO BETTER'). Use COUNTIF to count male students and female students in separate cells. Format all numbers to 1 decimal place. Save as Grade_Analysis.xlsx.

quiz.question: What is the correct syntax for an IF function?
quiz.explanation: IF requires three arguments: the condition to test, the value if true, and the value if false.

quiz.question: How would you write a nested IF to assign 'A' for 80+, 'B' for 70+, 'C' for 50+, and 'F' otherwise?
quiz.explanation: Nested IF statements check conditions from highest to lowest, assigning the first matching grade.

quiz.question: What does COUNTIF(B2:B20, 'M') do?
options: Adds all values in the range
quiz.explanation: COUNTIF counts how many cells in the range meet the specified condition, in this case containing 'M'.

quiz.question: When would you use AND inside an IF function?
options: When ALL conditions must be true for the result
When you want to count cells
When you want the highest number
quiz.explanation: AND requires every condition inside it to be true before the IF returns the true value.
